Name: Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Caterpie
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is covered with a green skin. When it grows, it sheds the skin, covers itself with silk, and becomes a cocoon.

Name: Metapod
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is in a temporary stage while making its body. It is almost completely unable to move on its own.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
This attack does 40 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Blue plant vines cloak the Pokémon’s identity in a tangled mass. It entangles anything that gets close.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

Name: Charizard
Ability-Effect: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are [R] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

Name: Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Combustion Blast during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Charizard-EX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

Name: Ninetales BREAK
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
BREAK-Rule: Ninetales BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Arcanine
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, you may move any number of [R] Energy attached to your Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that is described in Chinese legends. It is said to race at an unbelievable speed.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: Its body is light, and its legs are incredibly powerful. It can clear Ayers Rock in one leap.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Found near the mouth of a volcano. This fire-breather’s body temperature is nearly 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Blastoise-EX
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Blastoise-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Poliwag
Flavor-Text: Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its two legs are well developed. Even though it can live on the ground, it prefers living in water.

Name: Poliwrath
Effects: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An adept swimmer, it knows the front crawl, butterfly, and more. It is faster than the best human swimmers.

Name: Slowbro-EX
Effects: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Slowbro-EX
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused. During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Loll Roll Spin attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Seel
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Covered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of -40 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Dewgong
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its body is covered with a pure white fur. The colder the weather, the more active it becomes.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

Name: Starmie
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t choose a card you discarded with the effect of this Ability.)
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a geometric body. Because of its body, the locals suspect that it is an alien creature.

Name: Starmie BREAK
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon BREAK. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
BREAK-Rule: Starmie BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
You may discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its electric charges can reach even 100,000 volts. Careless contact can cause even an Indian elephant to faint.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A linked cluster formed of several Magnemite. It discharges powerful magnetic waves at high voltage.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A life-form whose identity is unknown. It is said to Screech or suddenly Self-Destruct.

Name: Electrode
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may Knock Out this Pokémon and attach it to one of your [L] Pokémon as a Special Energy card. This card provides 2 [L] Energy only while this card is attached to a Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. While it is flying, it makes crackling and snapping sounds.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its large ears are flapped like wings when it is listening to distant sounds. It extends toxic barbs when angered.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is easily angered. By swinging its well-developed horn wildly, it can even punch through diamond.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is recognized by its rock-hard hide and its extended horn. Be careful with the horn as it contains venom.

Name: Nidoking BREAK
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
BREAK-Rule: Nidoking BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Gastly
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack, discard an Energy attached to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A being that exists as a thin gas. It can topple an Indian elephant by enveloping the prey in two seconds.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: If you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, Haunter is there.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A descendant of the legendary animal baku, which is said to eat dreams. It is skilled at hypnotism.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon. If 1 of your Pokémon used Barrier during your last turn, this attack can’t be used.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon whose genetic code was repeatedly recombined for research. It turned vicious as a result.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Evolution Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It burrows and lives underground. If threatened, it curls itself up into a ball for protection.

Name: Diglett
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

Name: Machop
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Effects: This attack does 60 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

Name: Machamp BREAK
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 100 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
BREAK-Rule: Machamp BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Onix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: It usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.

Name: Hitmonchan
Flavor-Text: The spirit of a pro boxer has infused this Pokémon. It throws punches that are faster than a bullet train.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Its adorable appearance makes it popular as a pet. However, it is rare and difficult to find.

Name: Pidgeot-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Pidgeot-EX
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rattata
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its fangs are long and very sharp. They grow continuously, so it gnaws on hard things to whittle them down.

Name: Raticate
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 60 damage times the number of Special Energy cards in your opponent’s discard pile.
Flavor-Text: Its rear feet have three toes each. They are webbed, enabling it to swim across rivers.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Leek Slap during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It always walks about with a plant stalk clamped in its beak. The stalk is used for building its nest.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It lays several eggs a day. The eggs are apparently rich in nutrients and extremely delicious.

Name: Porygon
Effects: Choose [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], [M], [Y], or [N] type. The Defending Pokémon’s Weakness is now that type until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn’t change.)
Flavor-Text: Using the most advanced technologies, scientists finally succeeded in making the first artificial Pokémon.

Name: Dragonite-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 2 Basic Pokémon (except for Dragonite-EX) from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blastoise Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Blastoise-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Brock’s Grit
Description: Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Charizard Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Charizard-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Devolution Spray
Description: Devolve 1 of your evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can’t evolve this turn.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Maintenance
Description: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can’t shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can’t play this card.) Then, draw a card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Misty’s Determination
Description: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pidgeot Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Pidgeot-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Pokédex
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Oak’s Hint
Description: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Revive
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Slowbro Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Slowbro-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Super Potion
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Venusaur Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Venusaur-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Double Colorless Energy
Description: Double Colorless Energy provides [C][C] Energy.

Name: M Venusaur-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pidgeot-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Pidgeot-EX
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragonite-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 2 Basic Pokémon (except for Dragonite-EX) from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Brock’s Grit
Description: Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Misty’s Determination
Description: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Dr.オーヤマ考案「日米交流カード」パーティ気分で和洋せっちゅう。

Name: Flying Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: By learning how to fly, Pikachu overcame its weakness to Fighting Pokémon.

Name: Surfing Pikachu
Flavor-Text: One summer, a group of Pikachu was found riding the waves at the local beach.

Name: Imakuni?’s Doduo
Pok\u00e9mon-Power-Effect: When this Doduo retreats, hold this card and throw it as hard as you can because Doduo is running away. Throw the card horizontally with a snap to get the farthest distance!
Move-Energy: (This card cannot be used at official tournaments.)
Effects: From the moment you use this attack, you must begin to sing a song. (While the song is being sung, the game continues.) When the song is finished, this attack does 30 damage.

Name: Here Comes Team Rocket!
Description: Each player turns all of his or her Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).
